title: Trema eurhynchum
intro: Trema eurhynchum, also known as the "pigeon wing tree," is a species of plant in the family Salicaceae, characterized by its distinctive wing-shaped leaves and small, fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been found to aid in treating ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Trema eurhynchum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Trema eurhynchum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used to make teas, tinctures, and extracts, which are valued for their potential to promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Trema eurhynchum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Salicaceae. It is a member of the genus Trema, which consists of tropical and subtropical trees and shrubs. Trema eurhynchum is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia, and it is often found growing in woodland and riparian areas. The plant is a small to medium-sized tree or shrub, with simple leaves and small, greenish-white flowers. Trema eurhynchum is also known as the "white mangrove" or "water hawthorn," due to its ability to thrive in aquatic environments.
uses: Trema eurhynchum, also known as the tree of life, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its bark, leaves, and roots are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In some African countries, the plant is used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential antioxidant and hepatoprotective effects. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Trema eurhynchum for medicinal use.

constituents: Trema eurhynchum is known to contain various active constituents, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins. parts: Trema eurhynchum, commonly known as the mangrove trema, is a plant species found in tropical regions. preparations: Trema eurhynchum is reported to have various herbal preparations, including a decoction made from the plant's leaves and branches, which is used to treat fever and respiratory issues.
